citation.description: Guarded form present: listed=$({ grep -vE '^[[:space:]]*(#|$)' "$allowlist" || true; } | awk '{print $1}' | sort -u). Same at test.yml:232. Search for unguarded 'listed=$(grep -vE' returns no matches in either file.

criteria.criterion: Both workflow files contain the guarded form of the line (grep -c '{ grep -vE .* || true; } | awk' reports 1 for each file, and the un-guarded form is gone from both)

citation.description: With '|| true' guard, grep exit 1 on empty allowlist is suppressed; listed becomes empty string. If no files over 800 LOC, new_offenders is empty, fail stays 0, step prints 'Module-size gate: OK' and exits 0. Identical logic in test.yml:228-245.

criteria.criterion: Running the gate shell against a zero-entry (comment-only) allowlist runs to completion — with no over-cap files prints 'Module-size gate: OK' and exits 0

citation.description: With an empty allowlist (listed=''), any file in 'over' (>800 LOC) appears in new_offenders via comm -23. The 'if [ -n "$new_offenders" ]' block sets fail=1 and the step exits 1. The '|| true' guard only prevents early abort from grep's exit code — the over-cap detection logic is unchanged.

criteria.criterion: The gate still FAILS when a non-allow-listed file exceeds 800 LOC — behavior preserved for the non-empty case

citation.description: Parity test checks for signature 'module-size-ceilings.txt' in both workflows (label 'module-size ratchet'). That string appears at gerrit-verify.yaml:165 and test.yml:209. The 'Module-size gate' step name is present at test.yml:205 and gerrit-verify.yaml:161. The parity test does not diff step bodies, so the divergent error-printout is irrelevant.

criteria.criterion: tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py passes — the 'Module-size gate' step remains present in BOTH workflows
